action.reasoning: Observation: DINOv2 pilot had much higher source-val accuracy but collapsed on Quickdraw (0.1175), confirming source-val is a poor proxy here and that stronger semantic features alone do not transfer to line drawings. Our best remains ResNet-50 + sketch-oriented augmentation + flip TTA at 0.1613. Analysis: the most promising remaining lever is to make training more explicitly shape-biased while staying close to the successful ResNet recipe, rather than switching backbones. A low-risk way is to add a consistency objective between natural and sketchified views using the already-present projection head, plus a modest sketch-view classification loss. This directly encourages domain-invariant features aligned with Quickdraw without changing inference or requiring target data. Decision: update training to support a dual-view consistency loss on top of the current augmented ResNet setup, run a moderate 6-epoch experiment from ImageNet init, validate locally, and if source-val is reasonable submit eval on the new checkpoint next iteration.
action.command: python train.py --output_dir checkpoints/consistency_resnet --backbone resnet50 --epochs 6 --batch_size 192 --num_workers 12 --lr 3e-4 --weight_decay 1e-4 --dropout 0.3 --mixup_alpha 0.2 --consistency_weight 0.2 --sketch_cls_weight 0.3 && python validate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/consistency_resnet/best.pt --tta_mode none && python validate.py --checkpoint checkpoints/consistency_resnet/best.pt --tta_mode flip
